title: papaver nudicaule var. croceum
intro: Papaver nudicaule var. croceum, also known as the American Meadow Poppy or Western Meadow Poppy, is a variety of flowering plant in the poppy family. It is a perennial plant that produces bright yellow, orange, or red flowers, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
